name: Face Pull
resistance_profile: High-cable horizontal abduction/external rotation for rear delts and mid-traps.
cues: Pull to the face, elbows high
execution_steps: Set a cable pulley to just above head height and attach a rope.
Grab the rope with both hands, thumbs pointing back toward you, and step away until the cable is taut.
Pull the rope toward your face, driving your elbows high and out to the sides.
At the end, externally rotate so your knuckles face behind you, then return under control to the stretch.
good_when: You want to train rear delts and mid-traps together for shoulder health and posture.
You want a high-rep, joint-friendly accessory to finish a session.
You want external-rotation work built into a rear-delt movement.
bad_when: You want maximal rear-delt isolation — a reverse fly hits them more directly.
No cable or band is available.
common_errors: Going too heavy / using the back
